text:
The goal bearing is -43 degrees, which indicates the target is to the left (far_left). The distance is 7606 cm (far). According to navigation skill #7: "When far_left & far: prefer MOVE_FORWARD (avg +86cm progress, well-evidenced)." Although the target is to the left, moving forward is the recommended action to make progress rather than turning immediately or backtracking.

text:
The goal bearing is -152 degrees, which is behind and to the right (behind_right). The distance is 11110 cm (far). According to the recent experience: "When behind_right & far: MOVE_FORWARD → no change. Forward with behind_right bearing yielded no progress. Turn toward goal first." Also, experience #3 states: "When behind_right & far: TURN_LEFT → neutral (avg +0cm, seen 898x). [behind_right, far] TURN_LEFT → no change. Turned away from goal (bearing was behind_right). Turn the other way." This implies that turning left is incorrect when the goal is behind_right; instead, I should turn right to face the goal.

Therefore, the correct action is to turn right to align with the goal direction.
